title: Housing Search Assistance * Domestic Violence Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Search Assistance * Domestic Violence Issues 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Issues Domestic Violence Issues Former Foster Children Foster Children Hispanic/Latino Community Homeless Families Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues Teen Pregnancy Issues Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BRADLEY ANGLE HOUSING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing assistance. Assistance is based on funding availability and capacity. Assistance may involve helping survivors to: -Leave a dangerous place -Secure emergency safe nights and shelter access -Retain safe housing with eviction prevention -Transition to long-term or permanent housing -Emotional support, safety planning, and systems navigation support -Help with 14-day lease break letters -Address Confidentiality Program applications -Discussing housing options Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence within the last 180 calendar days. May offer additional services for Black, African-American, and LGBTQ2+ survivors of domestic violence when available. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm Email (503) 232-1528 303 Visit Website More Details CROSSROADS COMMUNITIES APPLEGATE LANDING HOUSING ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides listings of affordable housing providers and can offer connections to possible housing opportunities. 1875 Stoltz Hill Road Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Households who are at low-to-moderate income standards, survivors of domestic violence, and individuals with severe and persistent mental illnesses. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, Saturday 9am-5pm Email (541) 405-4210 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CROSSROADS COMMUNITIES HOUSING ADVOCACY
